interfaces.name: CardSuit
interfaces.signature: enum CardSuit: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case clubs, diamonds, hearts, spades }
interfaces.notes: Use 4 suits with standard deck rows; Joker has nil suit.

interfaces.name: CardRank
interfaces.signature: enum CardRank: Int, Codable, CaseIterable { case ace = 1, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, jack, queen, king, joker = 14 }
interfaces.notes: 13 ranks plus Joker identity for deterministic card-keying.

interfaces.name: Card
interfaces.signature: struct Card: Identifiable, Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let rank: Rank?; let suit: Suit?; let isJoker: Bool }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. isJoker == true implies rank == nil && suit == nil. Exactly 53 Card values in a fresh deck.

interfaces.name: GameMode
interfaces.signature: enum GameMode: String, Codable, Sendable { case sober, drinking }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Stored per TurnEvent for replay-fidelity; sober is default.

interfaces.name: EnginePhase
interfaces.signature: enum EnginePhase: Equatable { case idle, shielded, confirmArmed, revealed, jokerCapturePending, applyingRule, handoffLocked, gameOver }
interfaces.notes: UI binds directly to this; shielded is default and must be fail-safe on interruption.

interfaces.name: EndReason
interfaces.signature: enum EndReason: String, Codable, Sendable { case cupFull, roundLimitReached, deckExhausted }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. cupFull wins any simultaneous tie against deckExhausted, per prompt_contract.

interfaces.name: RuleDefinition
interfaces.signature: struct RuleDefinition: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { var soberText: String; var drinkingText: String }
interfaces.notes: AGREED by both round-3 outputs. Two genuinely distinct authored voices per card. Ruleset Editor shows one field bound to the active mode; forking/copying a ruleset seeds both fields so editing one mode never blanks the other.

interfaces.name: Ruleset
interfaces.signature: struct Ruleset: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; var name: String; var rules: [RuleKey: RuleDefinition]; let isBundledDefault: Bool; var createdAt: Date; var updatedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Must contain all 14 RuleKey cases with both text fields non-empty before Save is allowed.

interfaces.name: TurnEvent
interfaces.signature: struct TurnEvent: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let playerIndex: Int; let turnIndex: Int; let roundNumber: Int; let card: Card; let mode: GameMode; let ruleKey: RuleKey; let ruleTextSnapshot: String; let jokerCustomText: String?; let isCustomOrJokerRule: Bool; let unitsAssigned: Int; let timestamp: Date }
interfaces.notes: AGREED by both round-3 outputs, naming included. ruleTextSnapshot frozen at draw time. unitsAssigned (default 1, mode-neutral naming) is what 'most drinks/dares' sums. Cup-fill is never stored per-turn — derived as (kings drawn)/4.

interfaces.name: GameSessionRecord
interfaces.signature: struct GameSessionRecord: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let schemaVersion: Int; var name: String?; let startedAt: Date; let endedAt: Date; let players: [String]; let rulesetName: String; let endReason: EndReason; let timeline: [TurnEvent]; let funniestRule: FunniestRuleResult; var kingsDrawnCount: Int { get }; var cardsRemainingAtEnd: Int { get }; var mostDrinksPlayerIndex: Int? { get } }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. kingsDrawnCount/cardsRemainingAtEnd/mostDrinksPlayerIndex are computed from timeline (automatic Codable synthesis skips them; recomputed on decode) — makes 'recap can never diverge from turn events' true by construction.

interfaces.name: GameEngineState
interfaces.signature: struct GameEngineState: Equatable, Sendable { var phase: GamePhase; var deck: [Card]; var players: [String]; var ruleset: Ruleset; var mode: GameMode; var turnIndex: Int; var timeline: [TurnEvent] }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. No stored kingsDrawn field — derived from timeline, avoiding a second source of truth alongside GameSessionRecord's computed properties.

interfaces.name: TurnResolution
interfaces.signature: struct TurnResolution { let event: TurnEvent; let nextState: GameEngineState; let endReason: EndReason?; let requiresJokerCapture: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Returned from confirmReveal; engine never mutates UI directly.

interfaces.name: SceneLifecycleEvent
interfaces.signature: enum SceneLifecycleEvent: Sendable { case didEnterBackground; case willResignActive; case didBecomeActive; case orientationChanged; case rapidTapDetected }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Mapped from scenePhase + willResignActiveNotification/didBecomeActiveNotification at the app root; feeds PrivacyShieldCoordinator, not the engine directly.

interfaces.name: GameEngine
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class GameEngine { private(set) var state: GameEngineState; var hud: HUDSnapshot { get }; func start(players: [String], ruleset: Ruleset, mode: GameMode); func send(_ event: GameEngineEvent); func reset() }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Thin wrapper: calls GameEngineReducer.reduce, republishes state, dispatches returned effects. Lives in Engine/ folder; a build-phase script fails the build if any file under Engine/ imports CoreData, UIKit, or SwiftUI — enforces isolation without SPM package ceremony (the module-boundary fork from rounds 1-2 is now closed on this resolution).

interfaces.name: PersistedRulesetStore
interfaces.signature: protocol PersistedRulesetStore { func fetchAll() async throws -> [Ruleset]; func save(_ ruleset: Ruleset) async throws -> UUID; func delete(_ id: UUID) async throws; func fetchBundled() async throws -> Ruleset }
interfaces.notes: Used only in Setup and Ruleset Editor flows.

interfaces.name: GameHistoryStore
interfaces.signature: protocol GameHistoryStore: Sendable { func fetchAll() async throws -> [GameSessionRecord]; func fetch(id: UUID) async throws -> GameSessionRecord?; func save(_ session: GameSessionRecord) async throws; func delete(id: UUID) async throws }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. save is called exactly once, at game-end (triggered by GameEngineEffect.gameDidEnd), before Recap renders.

interfaces.name: RecapRenderer
interfaces.signature: protocol RecapRenderer { func renderImage(from session: GameSessionRecord) async throws -> UIImage }
interfaces.notes: Uses ImageRenderer output; no direct dependency on CoreData.

interfaces.name: PhotoPermissionService
interfaces.signature: enum PhotoPermissionService { static func requestAddOnlyAuthorization() async -> PHAuthorizationStatus; static func openSettingsDeepLink() }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. .addOnly only, never .readWrite — app never reads the library.

interfaces.name: ShareExportService
interfaces.signature: protocol ShareExportService { func presentShareSheet(image: UIImage, completion: @escaping (Bool, Error?) -> Void) }
interfaces.notes: Must be UIActivityViewController-backed to resolve completed/cancelled/failure states.

interfaces.name: AppRoute
interfaces.signature: enum AppRoute: Hashable, Sendable { case activeGame; case recap(RecapContext) }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Setup is the NavigationStack root, never a path case; RulesetEditor and both ModeSettings contexts are sheets. Path always resets to empty on cold launch.

interfaces.name: ScreenState
interfaces.signature: enum ScreenState<T, E: Error>: Sendable where T: Sendable { case empty; case loading; case success(T); case failure(E) }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Scoped to CRUD-shaped screens only — Ruleset Editor list load, History List fetch, Setup's ruleset-loading (E == PersistenceError). GamePhase and ExportOutcome keep their own richer, named types.

interfaces.name: PrivacyShieldCoordinator
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class PrivacyShieldCoordinator { private(set) var isForceShielded: Bool; func handle(_ event: SceneLifecycleEvent, engine: GameEngine?) }
interfaces.notes: AGREED by both round-3 outputs — closes the rounds 1-2 fork. Root-level, independent of GameEngine and of whatever sheet is presented. On any interruption: forwards .backgroundedOrInterrupted into the engine if a game is active, AND sets isForceShielded so the root view hard-blocks revealed content even if a Mode/Settings sheet (.midSession) is what's actually on screen.

interfaces.name: Suit
interfaces.signature: enum Suit: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case spades, hearts, diamonds, clubs }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Card-face rendering only, never used for rule lookup. Lives in Domain/, single app target, no SPM packages.

interfaces.name: Rank
interfaces.signature: enum Rank: Int, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case ace = 1, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, jack, queen, king }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. 13 standard ranks; Joker lives on Card.isJoker, not as a Rank case.

interfaces.name: RuleKey
interfaces.signature: enum RuleKey: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case ace, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, jack, queen, king, joker }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Ruleset lookup key; RuleKey(card:) maps a Card to its key including the joker case.

interfaces.name: Ruleset.isComplete
interfaces.signature: extension Ruleset { var isComplete: Bool { get }; var missingKeys: [RuleKey] { get } }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Ruleset Editor's invalid-mapping error state reads missingKeys to point at the unmapped row.

interfaces.name: FunniestRuleResult
interfaces.signature: enum FunniestRuleResult: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { case winner(turnEventID: UUID); case noCustomRulesPlayed }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Winner is a captured end-of-game ballot outcome (or default-to-last-custom-rule) — not derivable from timeline alone, so it's stored on GameSessionRecord unlike the other recap metrics.

interfaces.name: GameSessionRecord.init(from:endedAt:)
interfaces.signature: extension GameSessionRecord { init(from state: GameEngineState, endedAt: Date) }
interfaces.notes: Single mapping point from terminal engine state to persisted recap summary.

interfaces.name: GamePhase
interfaces.signature: enum GamePhase: Equatable, Sendable { case shielded(incomingPlayerIndex: Int); case confirmArmed(incomingPlayerIndex: Int); case revealed(TurnDraw); case jokerCapturePending(TurnDraw); case applyingRule(TurnDraw); case handoffLocked; case gameOver(GameSessionRecord) }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. shielded is default/universal fail-safe target; UI must early-return with no Card content instantiated while shielded, not a covered overlay. Every case is a plain value, directly constructible in a SwiftUI Preview.

interfaces.name: TurnDraw
interfaces.signature: struct TurnDraw: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let playerIndex: Int; let turnIndex: Int; let roundNumber: Int; let card: Card; let ruleText: String; let mode: GameMode }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. In-flight, not-yet-committed draw before it becomes a persisted TurnEvent.

interfaces.name: GameEngineEvent
interfaces.signature: enum GameEngineEvent: Sendable { case confirmReveal; case pass; case jokerTextSubmitted(String?); case skipJokerCapture; case backgroundedOrInterrupted; case modeToggled(GameMode) }
interfaces.notes: AGREED, resolving one small round-3 divergence: orientationChanged is intentionally NOT a GameEngineEvent case (Codex's draft had it duplicated on both this enum and SceneLifecycleEvent) — orientation only needs to reach PrivacyShieldCoordinator, not the engine directly, since shielding already covers it. backgroundedOrInterrupted is sent by PrivacyShieldCoordinator, not derived from raw scenePhase inside the engine.

interfaces.name: GameEngineReducer
interfaces.signature: enum GameEngineReducer { static func reduce(_ state: GameEngineState, _ event: GameEngineEvent) -> (state: GameEngineState, effects: [GameEngineEffect]) }
interfaces.notes: AGREED — the core fork from rounds 1-2 is closed. Pure, static, no side effects. Primary unit-test surface: deck exhaustion at every player count 2-6, cup-full-wins-tie, idempotent confirmReveal (5 rapid calls -> exactly 1 turn advance), funniest-rule default/empty paths.

interfaces.name: HUDSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct HUDSnapshot: Equatable, Sendable { let kingsDrawn: Int; let cardsRemaining: Int; let currentRoundDisplay: Int?; let currentPlayerName: String }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. currentRoundDisplay is nil when 10 rounds isn't mathematically reachable at the current player count.

interfaces.name: RulesetStore
interfaces.signature: protocol RulesetStore: Sendable { func fetchAll() async throws -> [Ruleset]; func save(_ ruleset: Ruleset) async throws; func delete(id: UUID) async throws }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Implemented by PersistenceController in Persistence/. Returns/accepts plain Ruleset values, never a managed object.

interfaces.name: PersistenceController
interfaces.signature: final class PersistenceController: RulesetStore, GameHistoryStore { static let shared: PersistenceController; init(inMemory: Bool = false); func bootstrapBundledRulesetIfNeeded() async throws }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. CoreData stack (RulesetEntity/RulesetCardEntity/GameSessionEntity/TurnEventEntity, schemaVersion from v1, background NSManagedObjectContext with viewContext auto-merge). inMemory:true backs the test target.

interfaces.name: RecapContext
interfaces.signature: enum RecapContext: Hashable, Sendable { case justFinished(sessionID: UUID); case historical(sessionID: UUID) }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Drives RecapView's Export+PlayAgain vs Export-only rendering; both load GameSessionRecord strictly from GameHistoryStore, never live GameEngine state.

interfaces.name: ModeSettingsContext
interfaces.signature: enum ModeSettingsContext: Sendable { case preGame; case midSession }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. preGame shows the reopen-onboarding link; midSession is reached from the Active Game HUD's small gearshape icon and omits it.

interfaces.name: ExportOutcome
interfaces.signature: enum ExportOutcome: Equatable, Sendable { case success; case cancelled; case permissionDenied; case failure(String) }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Maps UIActivityViewController's completionWithItemsHandler: completed==true -> success, completed==false && error==nil -> cancelled, error!=nil -> failure.

interfaces.name: ExportCoordinator
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class ExportCoordinator { private(set) var outcome: ExportOutcome?; func export(recap: GameSessionRecord) async }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Renders recap via ImageRenderer, requests PHPhotoLibrary .addOnly authorization before presenting UIActivityViewController, maps completion to ExportOutcome.

interfaces.name: AttestationService
interfaces.signature: struct AttestationService: Sendable { func hasAttested() -> Bool; func recordAttestation() }
interfaces.notes: AGREED IN SUBSTANCE — resolving a trivial round-3 signature diff (Codex's draft had recordAttestation() as async throws; kept as the simpler synchronous non-throwing form since it's a local UserDefaults write with nothing to await or fail on). UserDefaults-backed one-time reversible gate for drinking mode; toggling back to sober does not clear attestation.

interfaces.name: HapticsService
interfaces.signature: enum HapticsService { static func playConfirmReveal(); static func playKingOrCupFill() }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. No-ops silently if CHHapticEngine.capabilitiesForHardware().supportsHaptics is false. Called by GameEngine in response to GameEngineEffect, never directly by views.

interfaces.name: EmptyStateView
interfaces.signature: struct EmptyStateView: View { let title: String; let message: String; let systemImage: String }
interfaces.notes: AGREED — lane kept as polish_resilience (consistent across rounds 1-2 and the design_handoff component inventory); Codex's round-3 draft briefly moved it to primary_ui, resolved back for consistency. Shared across History List ("no games yet") and Recap's funniest-rule empty state.

interfaces.name: ErrorBanner
interfaces.signature: struct ErrorBanner: View { let message: String; let retry: (() -> Void)? }
interfaces.notes: AGREED — lane kept as polish_resilience for the same consistency reason as EmptyStateView. retry == nil renders a non-blocking dismiss-only banner; non-nil renders a Retry button.

interfaces.name: Ruleset.hasAllMappings
interfaces.signature: extension Ruleset { func hasAllMappings() -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Used by Ruleset Editor and validation paths before starting or saving.

interfaces.name: Ruleset.missingKeys
interfaces.signature: extension Ruleset { func missingKeys() -> [RuleKey] }
interfaces.notes: Returns explicit unmapped keys for focused validation UI.

interfaces.name: PersistenceError
interfaces.signature: enum PersistenceError: Error, LocalizedError { case notFound; case loadFailed(String); case saveFailed(String); case deleteFailed(String); case migrationFallbackUsed }
interfaces.notes: AGREED by both round-3 outputs. Canonical error type surfaced to ScreenState<T, PersistenceError> failures and ErrorBanner messages.

interfaces.name: GameEngineEffect
interfaces.signature: enum GameEngineEffect: Equatable, Sendable { case hapticConfirm; case hapticKingOrCupFill; case gameDidEnd(GameSessionRecord) }
interfaces.notes: AGREED by both round-3 outputs. Declarative signals returned alongside new state so the reducer stays pure — the @Observable wrapper is the only place that calls HapticsService or GameHistoryStore.save.

interfaces.name: Deck
interfaces.signature: enum Deck { static func standard53(shuffled: Bool = true) -> [Card] }
interfaces.notes: AGREED. Single source of the fixed 53-card deck (52 + Joker).
